name: The Masjid & the Adhan
epithet: Answering the call to the house of Allah
title: Masjid & Adhan Adhkar: the du'as for the mosque and the call to prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning
meta_description: The du'as for walking to the masjid, entering and leaving it, and what to say with the adhan, in Arabic, transliteration and English, each with its source.
keywords: masjid dua, dua entering mosque, dua leaving mosque, adhan dua, dua after adhan, wasilah dua, what to say after adhan, repeat after muadhdhin, Hisn al-Muslim, Buruja
lede: Five times a day the call goes out, and a believer answers it. Between hearing the adhan and standing in the rows, there is a path of words the Prophet ﷺ walked: light asked for on the way, mercy sought at the door, and a quiet du'a that the heavens do not turn back.
These are the adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer. They are how you answer the summons with your whole self, not just your feet.
intro: When the adhan is called, repeat each phrase after the muadhdhin, except at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where you say the words of seeking help from Allah. After the adhan, send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ and ask for his station of intercession, then make your own du'a, for the time between the adhan and the iqamah is not turned away.
Say the entering du'a as you step into the masjid, leading with your right foot, and the leaving du'a as you step out, leading with your left.
closing: Every step toward the masjid is written for you, one raising your rank and one wiping away a sin. To walk that path with these words on your tongue is to arrive already in worship, your heart turned before your body bows.
O Allah, open for us the doors of Your mercy, grant our Prophet ﷺ the station You promised him, and make us of those whose feet carry them to Your house in light.

faq.q: What do I say while the adhan is being called?
faq.a: Repeat each phrase quietly after the muadhdhin. The one exception is at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where instead you say La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah (there is no might nor power except by Allah).

faq.q: What should I say after the adhan finishes?
faq.a: Send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ, then ask Allah to grant him al-wasilah and the honoured station. The Prophet ﷺ said whoever asks this for him will be granted his intercession on the Day of Resurrection. Then make your own du'a, as the moment between adhan and iqamah is answered.

faq.q: Which foot do I lead with entering and leaving the masjid?
faq.a: Enter leading with your right foot and say the du'a asking Allah to open the doors of His mercy. Leave with your left foot and ask Allah for His favour, since for honoured places the right is given precedence on the way in.

faq.q: Why is the du'a between the adhan and iqamah special?
faq.a: Because the Prophet ﷺ told us that supplication made in that window is not rejected. It is a short, easily missed opening in the day where the door of response is held open, so do not let it pass without asking.

email.subject: Answering the call, all the way to the rows
email.alt_subject: What to say with the adhan and at the masjid
email.preview: The du'as for the masjid and the call to prayer, in Arabic, transliteration and meaning.
email.body:
Assalamu alaikum,

Five times a day the call goes out, and a believer answers it. Between hearing the adhan and standing in the rows, there is a path of words the Prophet ﷺ walked: light asked for on the way, mercy sought at the door, and a quiet du'a that the heavens do not turn back.

We have gathered the adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer, each with its Arabic, a transliteration to help you learn it, the meaning, and its source. Repeat after the muadhdhin, ask for the Prophet's ﷺ station after the call, and use the moment before the iqamah, the time when du'a is not refused.

Every step to the masjid is written for you. Walk it with these words and arrive already in worship:

social.hook:
There is a moment between the adhan and the iqamah when du'a is not refused.

Most of us let it pass in silence.
social.caption:
The adhkar of the masjid and the call to prayer, how you answer the summons with your whole self, not just your feet.

Repeat after the muadhdhin. Send blessings on the Prophet ﷺ and ask for his station of intercession. Then make your own du'a before the iqamah, the window the Prophet ﷺ said is never turned away. Enter with your right foot asking for mercy, leave with your left asking for His favour.

Save this, and read the full set with Arabic, transliteration and meaning at buruja.com/adhkar/masjid-adhan

#MasjidDua #AdhanDua #CallToPrayer #Dhikr #Dua #HisnAlMuslim #SunnahOfTheProphet #IslamicReminders #Buruja #Islam

source_note: Prophetic adhkar transcribed verbatim from Hisn al-Muslim (Sa'eed al-Qahtani) via sunnah.com; al-Bukhari and Muslim taken as sahih, jayyid/reliable as hasan. Recitation audio: the official Hisn al-Muslim audio (hisnmuslim.com) for the adhkar. Selection follows Hisn al-Muslim, with thanks to lifewithallah.com; framing is Buruja's own. FOR SCHOLAR REVIEW: confirm every Arabic text, reference, repetition count and grading before publication.

items.title: Light on the way to the masjid
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ اجْعَـلْ فِي قَلْبـي نُوراً ، وَفي لِسَـانِي نُوراً، وَفِي سَمْعِي نُوراً, وَفِي بَصَرِيِ نُوراً, وَمِنْ فََوْقِي نُوراً , وَ مِنْ تَحْتِي نُوراً, وَ عَنْ يَمِينيِ نُوراَ, وعَنْ شِمَالِي نُوراً, وَمْن أَماَمِي نُوراً, وَمِنْ خَلْفيِ نُوراَ, واجْعَلْ فِي نَفْسِي نُوراً, وأَعْظِمْ لِي نُوراً, وَعظِّمْ لِي نُوراً, وَاجْعَلْ لِي نُوراً, واجْعَلنِي نُوراً, أللَّهُمَّ أَعْطِنِي نُوراً, واجْعَلْ فِي عَصَبِي نُوراً, وَفِي لَحْمِي نُوراً, وَفِي دَمِي نُوراً وَفِي شَعْرِي نُوراً, وفِي بَشَرِي نُوراً أَللَّهُمَّ اجِعَلْ لِي نُوراً فِي قّبْرِي وَ نُوراَ فِي عِظاَمِي وَزِدْنِي نُوراً, وَزِدْنِي نُوراَ , وَزِدْنِي نُوراً وَهَبْ لِي نُوراً عَلَى نُوراً
items.translit: Allāhummaj'al fī qalbī nūran, wa fī lisānī nūran, wa fī sam`ī nūran, wa fī baṣarī nūran, wa min fawqī nūran, wa min taḥtī nūran, wa `an yamīnī nūran, wa `an shimālī nūran, wa min 'amāmī nūran, wa min khalfī nūran, waj`al fī nafsī nūran, wa 'a`ẓim lī nūran, wa `ẓẓim lī nūran, waj`allī nūran, waj`alnī nūran, Allāhumma 'a`tinī nūran, waj'al fī `aṣabī nūran, wa fī laḥmī nūran, wa fī damī nūran, wa fī sha`rī nūran, wa fī basharī nūran. [Allāhummaj`allī nūran fī qabrī wa nūran fī `iẓāmī.] [Wa zidnī nūran, wa zidnī nūran, wa zidnī nūran.] [Wa hab lī nūran `alā nūr.]
items.en: O Allah, place light in my heart, and on my tongue light, and in my ears light and in my sight light, and above me light, and below me light, and to my right light, and to my left light, and before me light and behind me light. Place in my soul light. Magnify for me light, and amplify for me light. Make for me light and make me a light. O Allah, grant me light, and place light in my nerves, and in my body light and in my blood light and in my hair light and in my skin light. [O Allah, make for me a light in my grave... and a light in my bones.] (At-Tirmidhi 5/483 (Hadith no. 3419).) [Increase me in light, increase me in light, increase me in light.] (Al-Bukhari in Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad (Hadith no. 695), p. 258. See also Al-Albani, Sahih Al-'Adab Al-Mufrad(no. 536).) [Grant me light upon light.] (Al-Bukhari, cf. Al-Asqalani, Fathul-Bari 11/118.)
items.note: The du'a the Prophet ﷺ made setting out for the prayer, asking for light in his heart, his senses, and every direction around him. A beautiful supplication to carry on the walk to Fajr.

items.title: Entering the masjid
items.arabic: أَعوذُ باللهِ العَظيـم وَبِوَجْهِـهِ الكَرِيـم وَسُلْطـانِه القَديـم مِنَ الشّيْـطانِ الرَّجـيم، [ بِسْـمِ الله، وَالصَّلاةُ وَالسَّلامُ عَلى رَسولِ الله]، اللّهُـمَّ افْتَـحْ لي أَبْوابَ رَحْمَتـِك
items.translit: 'A`ūdhu billāhi l-`aẓīm, wa bi-wajhihil-karīm, wa sultānihil-qadīm, min ash-shaytānir-rajīm. [Bismillāhi, wassalātu wassalāmu `alā rasūlillāhi.] Allāhummaftaḥ lī 'abwāba raḥmatik.
items.en: I seek refuge in Almighty Allah, By His Noble Face, By His primordial power, From Satan the outcast. [In the Name of Allah, and blessings and peace be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, open before me the doors of Your mercy.
items.ref: Abu Dawud 1/126, see also Al-Albani, Sahihul-Jami'As-Saghir 1/528
items.note: Step in with your right foot and ask Allah to open the doors of His mercy.

items.title: Leaving the masjid
items.arabic: بِسمِ الله وَالصّلاةُ وَالسّلامُ عَلى رَسولِ الله، اللّهُـمَّ إِنّـي أَسْأَلُكَ مِـنْ فَضْـلِك، اللّهُـمَّ اعصِمْنـي مِنَ الشَّيْـطانِ الرَّجـيم
items.translit: Bismillāhi waṣṣalātu wassalāmu 'alā Rasūlillāhi, Allāhumma 'innī 'as'aluka min faḍlika, Allāhumma`ṣimnī min ash-shaytānir-rajīm.
items.en: In the Name of Allah, and peace and blessings be upon the Messenger of Allah. O Allah, I ask for Your favor, O Allah, protect me from Satan the outcast.
items.note: Step out with your left foot and ask Allah for His favour, and for protection from the devil as you return to the world.

items.title: Repeating after the muadhdhin
items.arabic: يقول مثل ما يقول المؤذن إلا في حي على الصلاة ، وحي على الفلاح فيقول لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
items.translit: Lā ḥawla wa lā quwwata 'illā billāh.
items.en: There is no might nor power except by Allah. (You repeat what the muezzin says, except after 'Come to prayer, come to success,' when you say this instead.)
items.note: Echo each phrase of the adhan, except at 'come to prayer' and 'come to success', where you say there is no might nor power except by Allah.

items.title: After the two testimonies
items.arabic: وَأَنا أَشْـهَدُ أَنْ لا إِلـهَ إِلاّ اللهُ وَحْـدَهُ لا شَـريكَ لَـه ، وَأَنَّ محَمّـداً عَبْـدُهُ وَرَسـولُه، رَضيـتُ بِاللهِ رَبَّاً ، وَبِمُحَمَّـدٍ رَسـولاً وَبِالإِسْلامِ دينَـاً
items.translit: Wa 'anā 'ash-hadu 'an lā 'ilāha 'illallāhu waḥdahu lā sharīka lahu wa 'anna Muḥammadan 'abduhu wa rasūluhu, raḍītu billāhi rabban, wa bi Muḥammadin rasūlan wa bi 'l-islāmi dīnan.
items.en: I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, Who has no partner, and that Muhammad is His slave and His Messenger. I am pleased with Allah as my Lord, with Muhammad as my Messenger and with Islam as my religion. [To be recited in Arabic after the mu'adh-dhin's tashahhud or the words of affirmation of Faith]
items.note: Said in Arabic after the muadhdhin's shahadah: a declaration of being pleased with Allah as Lord, Muhammad ﷺ as Messenger, and Islam as religion. The Prophet ﷺ said the one who says it will have his sins forgiven.

items.title: Blessings on the Prophet ﷺ
items.arabic: يُصَلِّي عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعْدَ فَرَاغِهِ مِنْ إِجَابَةِ الْمُؤَذِّنِ
items.en: After replying to the call of Mu'adh-dhin. you should recite in Arabic Allah's blessings on the Prophet (ﷺ).
items.note: When the muadhdhin finishes, send your peace and blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ before making your own du'a.

items.title: Asking for the Prophet's ﷺ station
items.arabic: اللّهُـمَّ رَبَّ هَذِهِ الدّعْـوَةِ التّـامَّة وَالصّلاةِ القَـائِمَة آتِ محَـمَّداً الوَسيـلةَ وَالْفَضـيلَة وَابْعَـثْه مَقـامـاً مَحـموداً الَّذي وَعَـدْتَه [إِنَّـكَ لا تُـخْلِفُ الميـعاد]
items.translit: Allāhumma rabba hādhihi 'd-da'wati 't-tāmmah waṣ-ṣalāti 'l-qā'imah, 'āti Muhammadani 'l-wasīlata walfaḍīlata, wab 'ath-hu maqāma 'm-mahmūdani 'l-ladhī wa`adtahu, ['innaka lā tukhliful-mī'ād]
items.en: O Allah, Lord of this perfect call and established prayer. Grant Muhammad the intercession and favor, and raise him to the honored station You have promised him, [verily You do not neglect promises].
items.ref: Al-Bukhari 1/152, and the addition between brackets is from Al-Bayhaqi 1/410 with a good (Hasan) chain of narration
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ said whoever asks Allah to grant him al-wasilah and the honoured station after the adhan will be granted his intercession on the Day of Resurrection.

items.title: Du'a between the adhan and the iqamah
items.arabic: يَدْعُو لِنَفسِهِ بَيْنَ الْأَذَانِ وَالْإِقَامَةِ فَإِنَّ الدُّعَاءَ حِينَئِذٍ لاَ يُرَدُّ
items.en: Between the call to prayer and the 'Iqamah, you should supplicate Allah for yourself. Invocation during this time is not rejected.
items.note: The Prophet ﷺ taught that supplication in this window is not rejected. Use it: ask for what weighs on your heart before the prayer begins.
